programme.desc: A look at the hunt for an alternative suspect in the Essex Boys murders, uncovering explosive revelations that could turn the case on its head.

programme.title: Michael X: Hustler, Revolutionary, Outlaw
programme.desc: This is the story of the rise and fall of black rights activist Michael de Freitas, known as Michael X and once heralded as the UK's answer to Malcolm X.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Sidney Lumet, who was Oscar-nominated for his direction of 12 Angry Men, Dog Day Afternoon, Network and The Verdict.

programme.title: Discovering: Jason Robards
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor, who won Oscars for his roles in All the President's Men and Julia, and also starred in Melvin and Howard and Magnolia.

programme.title: The Sixties
programme.desc: A look at the events of 1968, which saw the assassinations of Robert F Kennedy and Martin Luther King Jr, as well as the escalation of the Vietnam War and civil rights protests.

programme.title: Discovering: Richard Dreyfuss
programme.desc: A glimpse into the life and career of the Oscar-winning Hollywood actor who starred in The Goodbye Girl, Jaws and Down and Out in Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of American film director Don Siegel, best known for 1956 sci-fi classic Invasion of the Body Snatchers and his work with Clint Eastwood, including Dirty Harry.

programme.title: Last Call: When a Serial Killer Stalked Queer New York
programme.desc: A sex worker becomes the killer's third victim. WWith the NYPD and the media under pressure to connect the murders, another piece of evidence leads investigators to Staten Island.

programme.title: Rolling Stone: Stories from the Edge
programme.desc: An inside look at the magazine's probing 1975 report on Patty Hearst's kidnappers, the arrival of Sex Pistols, and John Lennon's final days.

programme.title: Lockerbie
programme.desc: A Libyan double agent provides the evidence needed to bring the two suspects to court, but as the trial gets under way a key witness changes his story.

programme.title: Lancaster
programme.desc: The story of the bomber synonymous with the Dambusters, the night raids against Germany and the brave men and women who made them possible.

programme.title: The Two Escobars
programme.desc: How the fates of two Colombians - Andrés Escobar, the inspirational captain of Nacional, and Pablo Escobar, the notorious leader of the Medellin cartel - were permanently linked.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of American film director Don Siegel, best known for 1956 sci-fi classic Invasion of the Body Snatchers and his work with Clint Eastwood, including Dirty Harry.

programme.title: Discovering: Richard Dreyfuss
programme.desc: A glimpse into the life and career of the Oscar-winning Hollywood actor who starred in The Goodbye Girl, Jaws and Down and Out in Beverly Hills.

programme.title: UFO
programme.desc: When elite US Navy pilots encounter an unidentified aerial phenomenon in 2004, conversations around UFOs move from the fringes to the centre of the Pentagon.

programme.title: The Alien Autopsy Scandal
programme.desc: As the film is released across the globe to enormous fanfare, the footage is pored over by ufologists, journalists and the military to verify its authenticity, but Ray and Gary are hiding something.

programme.title: The Girl Who Caught a Killer
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rachael Watts shares her story of being kidnapped, strangled and left for dead as a child by Russell Bishop, the man later convicted of the Babes in the Wood murders.

programme.title: The Girl Who Caught a Killer
programme.desc: Part two of two. The testimony of seven-year-old Rachael Watts puts a killer behind bars and paves the way for a dramatic retrial for the Babes in the Wood murders.

programme.title: Brazilians in The Premier League
programme.desc: A look at the Brazilian stars who have graced the top flight.

programme.title: Going Clear: Scientology and the Prison of Belief
programme.desc: Alex Gibney's documentary, based on Lawrence Wright's book, sheds light on the controversial religious movement, from its roots in the mind of founder L Ron Hubbard through to its rise in popularity in Hollywood and beyond. The film also profiles eight former members of the Church of Scientology, including Oscar-winning screenwriter and director Paul Haggis, who offer their perspective on the acti

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: A leopard that has recently become the dominant predator of an area in Zambia's Luangwa Valley must now defend its hard-won territory.

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cherry blossoms bloom across Japan and whooper swans are called northward, signalling the arrival of spring in the Northern Hemisphere. In India a massive congregation of wild Asian elephants coincides with the colourful Holi Festival.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: A seal has a mysterious illness, Kayla finds a surprise in an attic full of baby raccoons and a former volunteer makes her debut as a veterinarian.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Tracing the brown bear's evolution from Ice Age giants to modern omnivores, and witnessing their bond with salmon and the intricate ecosystems that shape their survival.

programme.title: Islands
programme.desc: An exploration of the wildlife on Baffin in the Canadian Arctic, encountering arctic foxes, white beluga whales, a never-before-filmed two-tusked narwhal and more.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Albino orangutan Alba is released back into the wild, and one of the oldest orangutans in the centre is rushed into surgery with a life-threatening condition. Meanwhile, youngsters Beni and Lala finally return to class after an incident that left them with broken limbs, and Dilla unexpectedly gives birth during the night, but the delivery does not go to plan.

programme.title: Arctic Secrets
programme.desc: The Yukon River, in which thousands of salmon make the perilous trip to reach their spawning beds, dodging predators along the way.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: Deer season means the rehab is flooded with fawns. Chelsea tries to reunite one fawn with its mother, while two police officers rescue another.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: An infection threatens to sweep through the chimpanzees, prompting the keepers to begin urgent action to save their lives. Meanwhile, the overweight orangutans continue with their diet and the lemurs are introduced to the stump-tailed macaques before they move in together.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Young chimp Ben's mysterious bacterial infection causes him to lose a toe, while another primate in Hananya's house is struck down by the same illness. Capuchin TJ settles in well with the ladies and his South American housemates learn how to forage for food. Members of staff hide hard-boiled eggs for the capuchins to find and animal director Jeremy has a run-in with female orangutan Lingga.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Six-month-old Alejandra falls from a tree and badly damages one of her eyes. Clara and her one-year-old daughter Clarita are chosen for release back into the wild, but a flash flood threatens the mission. Meanwhile, the vets provide around-the-clock care for Erik, who has a dangerous infection, and attempt to reintroduce Dilla to her daughter Delilah.

programme.title: Arctic Secrets
programme.desc: Winter has arrived at the lower western shore of Hudson Bay, where polar bears reside. However, the ice that normally forms there is nowhere to be seen.

programme.desc: Wanting to be closer to the family, first-time homebuyers look for a place that won't break their budget and that's close to their favorite lakes; with houses selling for over a million dollars, they need a diamond in the rough to fix.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A retired military couple wants to move from Maryland to find a forever home n Michigan's Duck Lake. They're willing to put in some sweat equity for renovations with hopes they can inspire their children and grandchildren to make frequent visits.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: New parents look to escape their busy New York City lives with a move to Wolf Lake, a small, rustic lake community in upstate New York; the properties they consider require significant renovations and may break the bank.

programme.desc: A couple finds a fixer-upper in Wisconsin that overlooks Lake Michigan, and they're doing all the renovation work themselves; he's a contractor with experience renovating properties, and she's ready to join him and get her hands dirty.

programme.desc: The guys salvage five tobacco barns on one farm in Alton, Virginia, while Mark hunts for even more barns in Pennsylvania; Mark also visits an incredible finished home made of two cabins combined with a salt box addition.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders travel to Eagle Rock, Va., to build a dogtrot cabin in the Blue Ridge Mountains; Tim Rose travels to Halifax, Va., to see the finished log cabin they built five years earlier at the Springfield Distillery.

programme.title: Restored
programme.desc: Brett restores a classic Spanish Colonial Revival that hasn't been touched in years; his challenge is to make the kitchen and bath more functional while staying true to the home's 1920s character.

programme.title: Restored
programme.desc: A woman and her mother call on Brett to restore a 1927 Arts and Crafts house that's lost some character over the years; while restoring the exterior, kitchen and living room, Brett discovers a masterpiece hidden under all the added layers.

programme.title: Restored
programme.desc: Brett restores a 1913 Craftsman bungalow for a young couple with a baby on the way; he must find a way to bring back the natural wood in the house while making it bright and welcoming.

programme.title: Restored
programme.desc: Brett convinces a son and his mother to restore their beautiful 1901 shingle-style Victorian instead of selling it; the house suffers from a 1970s wood panel renovation, but Brett is determined to restore it back to its original glory.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A Texas couple teams up with two South African canvas tent experts to create their dream glamping experience. But they soon find the build is anything but luxurious. With rain, heat, and critters, they have to work together to reach the finish line.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A family builds thousands of earth blocks while withstanding winter monsoons, freezing temperatures and more to finish their forever home in San Antonio, Texas.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: Alaskan adventure guide Don Lee builds a remote survival cabin on top of a glacier, a site plagued with avalanches and unpredictable weather.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A family of four builds their dream home overlooking the Atlantic Ocean in Lubec, ME, while pro snowboarder Sean Busby and his wife build their off-grid home with stunning views of Glacier National Park.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Michael surprises Ashley with an 1893 raised cottage he purchased before seeing the interior; they find themselves in over their heads after realizing it's not quite the hidden gem Michael thought it was.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Michael finds a 1931 craftsman bungalow that's near the beach, and after seeing it, they know they have to buy it.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Ashley and Michael take on a house in the historic district; it's their first renovation since Ashley became pregnant, and they hope to complete the massive project before the baby arrives.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley save a family's two-story 1800s home from a flip that would have been a flop; they restore the original features while working to preserve the property's historical integrity.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Thirty years after purchasing their lakeside home, grandparents need more space than their 100-year-old camp can provide for their growing family.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Eldridge family owns a dilapidated camp on Lake Cobbosseeconntee, and the Cabin Masters have agreed to rescue the family camp and restore their good name, but as the team begins, they realize this little job has some big problems.
